title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
summary: RL Nabors from Arize argues that most applications can use small local models instead of frontier models, reducing latency, energy, and cost. He presents a framework using capability evals, golden datasets, and LLM-as-judge to measure when local models are sufficient and shares production examples including agentic systems, moderation classifiers, and summarization features.
writeup: RL Nabors of Arize makes the case that many applications can run on small local models rather than frontier models, cutting latency, energy use, and cost. The talk lays out an evaluation framework built on capability evals, golden datasets, and LLM-as-judge to determine when a local model is good enough for a given task. Production examples ground the argument: agentic systems, moderation classifiers, and summarization features. The takeaway is that the choice between local and frontier models becomes a measurable engineering decision rather than a default to the largest available model.
